name: La Serra de Segaria (Circular PR-CV 415)
region_intro: Hiking around Ondara offers diverse landscapes, from mountainous terrain to fertile valleys and coastal views. The region is characterized by features such as the Serra de Segaria and Sierra de Segària, providing challenging yet rewarding trails. Hikers can also explore expansive orange groves, ancient rock formations, and historical aqueduct pathways. Proximity to the Mediterranean Sea means some routes offer views of both the mountainous hinterland and the coast. Best hiking trails around Ondara The most popular hiking route is La Serra de Segaria (Circular PR-CV 415), a 6.8 miles (10.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 26 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the mountainous terrain of the Serra de Segaria. Another top favourite among local hikers is Dark Cave – Summit of Sierra de Segària loop from Beniarbeig, a moderate 4.1 miles (6.6 km) path. This trail ascends to the summit of Sierra de Segària, offering panoramic views. Local hikers also love the Old Aqueduct Pathway – Benimeli Well loop from Beniarbeig, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail leading through valleys and past historical infrastructure, often completed in about 2 hours 20 minutes. Hiking around Ondara is defined by mountainous terrain, lush valleys, and historical aqueduct pathways. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls through orange groves to more demanding ascents. The routes in Ondara are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1,800 reviews. More than 7,700 hikers have used komoot to explore Ondara's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Ondara?
A: Ondara offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll find mountainous terrain, particularly in the &lt;strong&gt;Serra de Segaria&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Segària&lt;/strong&gt;, alongside lush valleys, expansive orange groves, and fertile fields. Some routes also provide views of the Mediterranean Sea, ancient rock formations, and canyons.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Ondara?
A: Yes, Ondara has several easy trails. For less strenuous options, consider the aqueduct pathways like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10355957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Aqueduct Pathway – Benimeli Well loop from Beniarbeig&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14677944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Aqueduct Pathway – Trail to Parc Prue Begari loop from Beniarbeig&lt;/a&gt;. These routes are generally accessible and suitable for various fitness levels, including families.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in the Ondara area?
A: There are over 20 hiking routes available around Ondara on komoot, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. This includes 6 easy, 13 moderate, and 1 difficult trail.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Ondara?
A: Yes, many routes in Ondara are circular. A notable example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e867704308/la-segarra-de-segaria-circular-pr-cv-415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Serra de Segaria (Circular PR-CV 415)&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate challenge through mountainous terrain. Another popular circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31488642" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dark Cave – Summit of Sierra de Segària loop from Beniarbeig&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of historical or cultural sights can I see on a hike?
A: Many trails in Ondara offer glimpses into the region's history. The Old Aqueduct Pathways, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10355957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Aqueduct Pathway – Benimeli Well loop from Beniarbeig&lt;/a&gt;, showcase historical infrastructure amidst natural beauty. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/964080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgos Riverbed and Aqueduct in Gata de Gorgos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails with good viewpoints or panoramic vistas?
A: Absolutely. The mountainous areas provide excellent viewpoints. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31488642" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dark Cave – Summit of Sierra de Segària loop from Beniarbeig&lt;/a&gt; ascends to the summit of Sierra de Segària, offering panoramic views of the surrounding landscape, including the coast.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Ondara?
A: The hiking routes in Ondara are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1,800 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from challenging mountain paths to serene aqueduct trails, and the beautiful natural scenery.

Q: Are there any trails that follow old railway lines or greenways?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/424535" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dénia to Gandía Greenway&lt;/a&gt; is a converted old railway line that offers a flat and scenic route. It passes through orange groves, fields, and over streams, providing views of both mountains and the sea. It's ideal for walking and cycling.

Q: Can I find trails that lead to natural attractions like canyons or rivers?
A: Yes, the region features several natural attractions. You can explore areas with canyons, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6380613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranc de Teulada&lt;/a&gt;, or follow riverbeds like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/964080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgos Riverbed and Aqueduct in Gata de Gorgos&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2615829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mouth of the Molinell River&lt;/a&gt; is another natural highlight.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Ondara?
A: Ondara is suitable for hiking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for longer hikes. Winter can also be a good time for hiking, with cooler weather. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended during this season.

Q: Are there any trails that connect to nature parks or protected areas?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/424535" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dénia to Gandía Greenway&lt;/a&gt; connects to the &lt;strong&gt;Marjal de Pego-Oliva Nature Park&lt;/strong&gt;, an important wetland area known for its rice fields, diverse birdlife, and canals. This offers a different natural environment to explore.
tips: Medium difficulty is an understatement! We are in good shape and really fit, but this tour was difficult. Partly there were no paths, it was more of a climbing tour on the mountainside. Good shoes and a head for heights are a must!

One of the panoramic points par excellence in the Marina Alta. The views are amazing in all directions, you will be able to appreciate the Marjal de Pego-Oliva, the Montgó and the beaches of Denia among other places. The mountain range has at the same time a great cultural, historical and tourist value.
